jokes.text: Michael answering phone with 'Michael Bluth, residential complexes' while driving a Google Street View car
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to work in 'high-tech' that 'collides with real estate'
jokes.punchline: He's literally driving around taking pictures of houses for Google
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor for Michael's delusions of grandeur - he's literally just taking pictures of houses while claiming to be in high-tech

jokes.text: Take my picture!
jokes.setup: Michael is trying to have a professional phone conversation about business
jokes.punchline: Random person interrupts demanding photos be taken
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist interruption, gets a mild laugh but nothing special

jokes.text: I think we got an ostrich. Oh, God, grab him.
jokes.setup: Normal office conversation about cars
jokes.punchline: Sudden inexplicable ostrich emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic AD random escalation - completely unexpected animal emergency in office setting

jokes.text: Michael driving his old stair car saying 'I'm used to a car with some stares'
jokes.setup: Being told the Google car will get him 'some stares'
jokes.punchline: Michael mishears/twists it to reference his old stair car that literally had stairs
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay that calls back to iconic visual element while advancing current story

jokes.text: Meta joke about not being able to show the Google car because it's their property, then blurring it anyway
jokes.setup: Show explaining they can't show Google's car for legal reasons
jokes.punchline: They blur it out anyway, making the explanation pointless
jokes.explanation: Clever meta commentary on TV production constraints and corporate legal issues

jokes.text: Barry being unclear about Ron Howard meeting: 'Apparently, he directed a movie called Cocoon'
jokes.setup: Michael asking for more information about why Ron Howard wants to meet
jokes.punchline: Barry gives completely irrelevant trivia about Ron's filmography instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Barry incompetence - completely missing the point of Michael's question

jokes.text: Barry offering to tell Ron Howard to go (BLEEP) himself while in front of a jury
jokes.setup: Barry being crude and unprofessional on the phone
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's saying all this in front of a jury during a trial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - completely inappropriate behavior in a professional setting

jokes.text: Barry's courtroom demonstration: 'I can't reach the Chachi'
jokes.setup: Barry demonstrating he can't reach a school gate latch
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces 'latch' as 'Chachi' (Happy Days reference)
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Barry malapropism with bonus Happy Days reference for Ron Howard connection

jokes.text: Bob Loblaw Law Bomb sequence
jokes.setup: Barry making a legal pun: 'If he can't reach, this trial's a breach'
jokes.punchline: Bob Loblaw responds with 'A Bob Loblaw Law Bomb' - tongue twister escalation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalation that became a series catchphrase - perfect economy and rhythm

jokes.text: Kitty's 'D girl' misunderstanding with Michael
jokes.setup: Kitty saying she's a 'D girl'
jokes.punchline: Michael assumes it's sexual, she clarifies it means 'development' executive
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection playing on both characters' histories and Hollywood terminology

jokes.text: You're probably going to call me a pig for this, too, but are you sure that you meant to say 'knew'?
jokes.setup: Kitty says she 'knew people' to get the job
jokes.punchline: Michael delicately suggests she meant sexual favors
jokes.explanation: Michael's diplomatic way of making crude suggestion while acknowledging Kitty's sensitivity

jokes.text: The narrator's clarification that Kitty only 'blew smoke' at Imagine
jokes.setup: Previous joke about Kitty's methods
jokes.punchline: Narrator correcting with literal 'blew smoke' reference
jokes.explanation: Clever narrator intervention that plays with the innuendo

jokes.text: Maeby asking 17-year-old Kitty to buy her booze
jokes.setup: Maeby working as a film executive despite being 17
jokes.punchline: She asks her adult assistant to buy her alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Arrested Development absurdity - adult-child power dynamic inversion

jokes.text: Maeby asking Kitty to buy her booze and drugs at age 17
jokes.setup: Maeby as a teenage film executive
jokes.punchline: Casually requesting illegal substances from her assistant
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that perfectly captures the show's absurd Hollywood satire

jokes.text: Kitty offering to get 'smack or hash or Special K'
jokes.setup: Kitty agreeing to help Maeby with 'whatever else'
jokes.punchline: She casually offers hard drugs to a teenager
jokes.explanation: Kitty's complete inappropriateness escalated to absurd criminal territory

jokes.text: She didn't even get releases from her family. ...that Maeby's was one of the bodies that was buried.
jokes.setup: Kitty criticizing Maeby's work on family project
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - Maeby's career was 'buried' like the bodies she helped hide
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that connects literal and metaphorical 'body burying' in classic AD style

jokes.text: Jerry Bruckheimer building height rivalry explanation
jokes.setup: Explaining building heights in Beverly Hills
jokes.punchline: Petty Hollywood producer dick-measuring via building heights
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Hollywood ego and competitiveness through architectural metaphor

jokes.text: Multiple Ron Howard lookalikes casting for Andy Griffith Show
jokes.setup: Meeting Ron Howard in his office
jokes.punchline: He's casting multiple versions of himself for Andy Griffith Show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about Ron Howard's Opie Taylor past and Hollywood vanity

jokes.text: Hey, nice try, mister. We're never going to beat the original.
jokes.setup: Child actor seeing Michael pass by
jokes.punchline: Kid dismissively comparing Michael to 'original' Ron Howard
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about casting process while showing kid's adult-like industry cynicism

jokes.text: Hang on a second. You guys finally making that movie? I've been reading about it for, like, 40 years.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions Andy Griffith Show project
jokes.punchline: Michael noting the project's decades-long development hell
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire about projects stuck in eternal development

jokes.text: Moon landing conspiracy reveal in the LEM
jokes.setup: Ron taking Michael into the lunar module for privacy
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he witnessed the faked 1969 moon landing as a child
jokes.explanation: Audacious conspiracy theory told matter-of-factly, perfectly fitting AD's reality-bending style

jokes.text: Ron Howard's movie inspirations from random photos
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his movies come from inspiring photographs
jokes.punchline: Splash from counterfeit Hockney, Da Vinci Code from random photo, Willow from coupon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity that satirizes Hollywood pretension while being surprisingly believable

jokes.text: But I never had a face to put on it. Until now. You.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his interest in the market crash story
jokes.punchline: Dramatically pointing at Michael as his poster child for suffering
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hollywood's callous commodification of personal tragedy

jokes.text: I can see why you're telling me in the LEM, huh?
jokes.setup: Michael thinking Ron is confessing affair
jokes.punchline: References secretive location while completely misunderstanding the situation
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Michael's completely wrong assumption makes him complicit in imagined conspiracy

jokes.text: Ron Howard father/daughter vs mistress confusion
jokes.setup: Ron saying 'my girl Rebel' and being secretive about it
jokes.punchline: Michael assumes she's his mistress when she's actually his daughter
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that sets up major plot complications while being perfectly logical

jokes.text: Ed Harris not being right for Barney Fife
jokes.setup: Ron having a tough meeting coming up
jokes.punchline: Telling Ed Harris he's 'simply not a Barney'
jokes.explanation: Perfect casting absurdity - intense dramatic actor for bumbling comedic role

jokes.text: Health plan benefits revelation: 'Health plan is not another one, by the way. No health plan.'
jokes.setup: Michael excited about producer perks
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation about lack of health benefits
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Hollywood glamour with practical reality

jokes.text: Oh! If you don't sign, you will be fine. Hey.
jokes.setup: Discussion about George Sr not signing things
jokes.punchline: Rhyming catchphrase about avoiding signatures
jokes.explanation: Characteristic George Sr. rhyming wisdom that's both silly and practically sound for a criminal

jokes.text: George Sr.'s lack of signature leading to money order gifts
jokes.setup: George Sr. claiming he doesn't have a signature
jokes.punchline: This explains why all his gifts are money orders instead of cards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail that retroactively explains years of behavior with perfect logic

jokes.text: Can you feel your hands? I can't feel my pinky or this one.
jokes.setup: Context of nerve damage or circulation problems
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of losing feeling in multiple fingers
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy, works mainly as character detail about George Sr's health issues

jokes.text: It's not too embarrassing walking around with a bunch of photographs of yourself. Well, looks like it's part of your job. You're an actress? No, I'm a narcissist.
jokes.setup: Michael noting her carrying headshots
jokes.punchline: Rebel's blunt self-assessment as narcissist rather than actress
jokes.explanation: Sharp, self-aware humor that immediately establishes Rebel's personality and honesty

jokes.text: Meet-cute cliché conversation during actual meet-cute
jokes.setup: Rebel complaining about ridiculous meet-cute audition clichés
jokes.punchline: She's literally having a meet-cute with Michael while describing how fake they are
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta joke that simultaneously executes and deconstructs the romantic comedy trope

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? I don't even know what that is. I should've... Sorry. That's funny. I should have said, 'Where?' Of course, I know what it is.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to know the Ealing Club
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting he doesn't know, then awkwardly backtracking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to seem sophisticated but immediately undermining himself

jokes.text: Hey, I met a girl today and her name is (BLEEP)
jokes.setup: Michael excited about meeting Rebel
jokes.punchline: Realizes he never got her name, proving her earlier point about meet-cutes
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier meta-joke - Michael falls into exact trope Rebel criticized

jokes.text: Michael realizing he forgot to get the girl's name
jokes.setup: Michael walking away after successful meet-cute
jokes.punchline: Two blocks later realizing he never got her name, just like the cliché they discussed
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the meta joke - Michael becomes the cliché he just heard criticized

jokes.text: We have to keep this quiet! Dad? No, no. Don't say I'm down here. Don't acknowledge me.
jokes.setup: Michael spots his father unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: George Sr. panicking about being seen in wrong apartment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr's secretive nature and fear of being caught

jokes.text: George Sr. in wrong Lucille's apartment looking for suits
jokes.setup: George Sr. claiming he's lost his direction in the desert
jokes.punchline: He's in Lucille Austero's apartment instead of Lucille Bluth's
jokes.explanation: Classic George Sr. confusion with perfect callback to ongoing series relationships

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I was in the desert, and I've lost my sense of direction.
jokes.setup: George Sr. caught in wrong apartment
jokes.punchline: Blaming desert for navigational confusion indoors
jokes.explanation: Good George Sr. deflection - absurd excuse delivered with straight-faced confidence

jokes.text: Office ceiling height competition between producers
jokes.setup: Explaining why Michael's office ceiling is lower
jokes.punchline: Ron, Brian, and Jerry Bruckheimer in escalating ceiling height wars
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Hollywood pettiness to architectural absurdity

jokes.text: Kitty's 'not the hot tub at Bruckheimer Tower' comment
jokes.setup: Michael asking for help finding an actress on a couch
jokes.punchline: Kitty referencing Bruckheimer's notorious hot tub casting sessions
jokes.explanation: Sharp Hollywood insider joke about casting couch culture with specific producer reference

jokes.text: Carl Weathers directing crime reenactment show about himself
jokes.setup: Carl never getting rights for his crime reenactment series
jokes.punchline: He made himself the subject of the final episode
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carl Weathers move - turning legal troubles into content opportunity

jokes.text: I guarantee you, give me an old Royal and a glass of Scotch, and I'll give you 250 pages where the lightning hits the tree. That's not what we do here. I mean, where the drop hits the pond. That's it. Then the yellow robot gets mad at the pink robot... Correction. The purple robot.
jokes.setup: Stefan promising quality screenplay
jokes.punchline: Increasingly confused metaphors and robot storylines
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Stefan's pretentious but confused writing style

jokes.text: Warden Gentles' confused Hollywood pitch meetings
jokes.setup: Warden trying to pitch scripts in Hollywood meetings
jokes.punchline: Mixing up his pitches: 'yellow robot gets mad at pink robot... correction, purple robot'
jokes.explanation: Perfect fish-out-of-water comedy with prison warden trying to navigate Hollywood

jokes.text: Might I suggest bringing in someone younger to play the father? A Philip Seymour Hoffman type? A Philip Seymour Hoffman type. And so, naturally, I thought of you.
jokes.setup: Stefan suggesting Philip Seymour Hoffman type for father role
jokes.punchline: Tobias thinking he resembles the acclaimed actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias delusion - thinking he's similar to a respected dramatic actor

jokes.text: Conan O'Brien's note to Andy Richter about not responding
jokes.setup: Conan giving Andy notes about his talk show performance
jokes.punchline: Telling Andy it's funnier when he doesn't respond and audience thinks he's stupid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about late-night TV dynamics and Andy's actual comedic persona

jokes.text: Ron Howard thinking Carl Weathers is Cuba Gooding Jr. in makeup
jokes.setup: Michael presenting Carl Weathers for the Carl Weathers role
jokes.punchline: Ron thinks it's Cuba Gooding Jr. with 'perfect Carl Weathers makeup'
jokes.explanation: Absurd Hollywood racism/confusion about Black actors, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Orange County Imagine mistaken for psychiatric facility
jokes.setup: Michael working at Orange County Imagine office
jokes.punchline: Sign confusion leads psychiatric patients to the production office
jokes.explanation: Classic AD visual gag about institutional similarity and Michael's declining circumstances

jokes.text: George Sr.'s Mexican porn/language learning gift
jokes.setup: George Sr. bringing Michael a peace offering gift
jokes.punchline: It's Mexican porn he claims is for language learning
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Sr. inappropriate gift with thin excuse for his Mexican living situation

jokes.text: Do you know a guy named Herbert Love? I know a lot of people, sure, 'cause I'm a big producer now. He ate a mouse.
jokes.setup: George Sr. asking about someone named Herbert Love
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that this person ate a mouse
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist detail dropped casually into conversation

jokes.text: Ron Howard's father-son relationship clarification
jokes.setup: Michael thinking the movie focuses on him and his father
jokes.punchline: Ron clarifies it's about Michael and his son - 'You're the father'
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist delivered with perfect economy - completely reframes Michael's understanding

jokes.text: Rebel being revealed as a bagpipe player
jokes.setup: Michael finding the mysterious girl at the Ealing Club
jokes.punchline: She's playing bagpipes - the most unromantic possible instrument
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-romantic reveal - bagpipes being the least sexy possible musical choice

jokes.text: The narrator's commentary on bagpipe music: 'hard to tell if it was good music played horribly or horrible music played well'
jokes.setup: Rebel playing bagpipes
jokes.punchline: Perfectly describing the ambiguity of bagpipe quality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor with perfect structure and universal relatability

jokes.text: Michael's son age lie progression
jokes.setup: Rebel saying her son is 6, Michael saying his is 7
jokes.punchline: Michael 'splitting the difference' to teen, 17 - completely illogical math
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic fail - 'splitting the difference' between 7 and 17 makes no sense

jokes.text: Michael realizing he's dating Ron Howard's daughter
jokes.setup: Michael successfully hooking up with Rebel
jokes.punchline: Seeing her headshot reveals she's Ron Howard's daughter, not mistress
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier misdirection with major plot implications

jokes.text: Narrator's correction and follow-up: 'Actually she's his daughter. But that's kind of worse, don't you think?'
jokes.setup: Michael's horror at dating boss's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actually worse - she's his daughter
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the situation worse and directly addresses the audience

jokes.text: Rocky Richter's red hair wig joke to Conan
jokes.setup: Rocky filling in for Andy on Conan's show
jokes.punchline: Threatening to rip off Conan's 'red rug' and turn him into Ron Howard
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback that ties Conan's hair to Ron Howard while getting biggest laugh of Andy's career
